denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/08/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102732055          Group #: GRP-1345
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-151286
Claim #: CLM-2026-206286
Date of Service (requested): 04/18/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 04/20/2026 for Septal myectomy evaluation related to Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Septal myectomy evaluation
Diagnosis (as submitted): Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0125 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0125 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Aetna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Septal myectomy evaluation. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2).

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102732055) and reference UM-2026-151286. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Septal myectomy evaluation recommended as the next appropriate step. LVOT gradient 65 mmHg on stress echo with NYHA class III symptoms. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
